title: DEPOT TO DEPOT
subtitle: The Midnight Bus · 11:14 PM · Tuesday Run
epigraph_upright: Tuesday's run. The depot. Headlights on. Parking brake off. The late-shift worker is already boarded — they came in five minutes early because they always do. The bus is itself. The road is the road.
epigraph_reversed: The bus runs out of fuel two miles short. The late-shift worker rides past their stop because Cora can't get to the mirror. The route runs long.
scene_description: Standard Tuesday run. Miles 0, fuel 4. The late-shift worker is on the bus. The first passenger boards at mile 1. The lost traveler at mile 2. The old regular at mile 3. Cora at the depot when she swaps out.
direction_hint: Drive to 6 miles (the destination). Connect with 3 passengers. Leap at the DESTINATION threshold.
opening_log_lines: the depot lights are bright. the bus has been idling for fifteen minutes.
the late-shift worker is already in aisle middle. they nod when you sit down.
the dashboard radio is between two stations.

stagnation_thresholds.label: a passenger has been waiting for the next stop.

doubt_thresholds.label: cora at the wheel, fresh thermos coffee.
she's not sure she took the right exit.

notes: Easy-mode Chariot. Standard Tuesday run. 6 miles to destination, 4 starting fuel. The late-shift worker is preoccupied (hard mood); the old regular is lonely.
ambient_pool: the road sound shifts under the wheels.
the dashboard radio finds a half-station.
the CB on channel 17 has somebody talking about the weather.
